title: Overtune
description: Overtune is a clean in-game music player for Minecraft. Browse, play, queue, and organize Minecraft’s soundtrack without leaving the game.
body:
# **_Overtune_** 

**A polished in-game music player for Minecraft.**

It gives you full control over the game’s soundtrack with a clean, dark, Spotify-inspired interface built directly into Minecraft.

Browse every built-in Minecraft music track by song, album, artist, playlist, or queue. Play tracks on demand, pause, skip, shuffle, repeat, adjust volume, like songs, and build your own playlists without leaving the game.

**_Overtune_** also supports imported songs. Drop your own audio files into the import folder, convert them in-game when needed, and add them to your library alongside Minecraft’s original music.

## Features:

- Full in-game music player that looks like that one green platform
- Built-in Minecraft soundtrack library
- Songs, albums, artists, liked songs, playlists, and queue
- Import custom songs
- MP3, FLAC, WAV, M4A, AAC, Ogg Opus, and Ogg FLAC conversion support
- Ogg Vorbis direct playback
- Custom playlists with saved local config
- Now-playing toast notifications
- Independent music player volume
- Shuffle, repeat, pause, skip, and seek controls
- Event-based music for moments like rain, danger, boss fights, first Nether entry, first Elytra flight, credits, and more
- Custom song assignments for each event
- Vanilla music suppression so tracks do not overlap
- Keybind support
- Clean dark UI designed to feel natural inside Minecraft

**_Overtune_** turns Minecraft’s music into something you can actually control, customize, and experience on your own terms.

Got any ideas? Message @DdiceW on DC.
